tokens.v: I believe older students, ages 14 to 18, benefit more from small-group learning,
the reason is not that teens try harder. Group work pays off
members bring different knowledge to the table,
that difference is what young children have not yet built up.
high school, students have started to specialize. One classmate has read a lot of history, another can code, a third is good at debate.
they work on the same project, each adds an angle the others do not have,

the final result is better than what any one of them could do alone.
Young children,
Claire says, are eager and willing to cooperate.
at ages 5 to 11, their knowledge is still very similar,
group work often turns into everyone saying the same thing with more noise.
the real rewards of group learning belong mainly to older students.

rounds.name: Round 4 · 句法骨架
rounds.description: 整块从句 / 介词短语 / 修饰结构

tokens.v: I believe older students, ages 14 to 18, benefit more from small-group learning, and

tokens.answer: the reason is not that teens try harder.

tokens.v: Group work pays off

tokens.answer: when members bring different knowledge to the table,

tokens.v:
and that difference is what young children have not yet built up.
By high school, students have started to specialize. When they work on the same project,

tokens.answer: each adds an angle the others do not have,

tokens.v: and the final result is better than

tokens.answer: what any one of them could do alone.

tokens.v: That is exactly when

tokens.answer: a group is worth more than the sum of its parts.

tokens.v: Young children, as Claire says, are eager and willing to cooperate. But at ages 5 to 11,

tokens.answer: their knowledge is still very similar,

tokens.v:
so group work often turns into everyone saying the same thing with more noise. Cooperation without difference gives you warmth, not depth.
For this reason,

tokens.answer: the real rewards of group learning — arguing, checking each other, filling in blind spots —

tokens.v: belong mainly to older students.
